content.full_text:
State of California 
Memorandum 
To 
Advice File: 
Section 820l3(c) 
Date 
Dec. 4, 1984 
No. M-84-3l4 
From 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
Carla Wardlow 
Subject: 
Reporting by Parners and Partnerships 
Individuals owning a 50 percent or greater interest in a 
partnership must cumulate their personal contributions with 
those of the partnership for purposes of determining whether 
major donor statements must be filed. 
One Form 461 should 
be filed containing the names of the partnership and the 
partner(s). 
The statement should also indicate which payments 
were made by each contributor. 
Partners owning less than 50 percent of a partnership will 
cumulate their contributions with those of the partnership 
only if they are acting as a "combination of persons" which 
is attempting to influence the voters for or against the 
nomination or election of a candidate or the passage or 
defeat of a measure.

sections.conclusion: Individuals owning a 50 percent or greater interest in a partnership must cumulate their personal contributions with those of the partnership for purposes of determining whether major donor statements must be filed. One Form 461 should be filed containing the names of the partnership and the partner(s). The statement should also indicate which payments were made by each contributor. Partners owning less than 50 percent of a partnership will cumulate their contributions with those of the partnership only if they are acting as a "combination of persons" which is attempting to influence the voters for or against the nomination or election of a candidate or the passage or defeat of a measure.
sections.question_synthetic: How should partners report contributions when they own interests in a partnership, and must they cumulate their personal contributions with partnership contributions for major donor statement filing purposes?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Partners with 50% or greater ownership must cumulate personal and partnership contributions for major donor statement filing on a single Form 461. Partners with less than 50% ownership only cumulate contributions if acting as a coordinated "combination of persons" to influence elections or measures.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a memorandum to advice file without traditional QUESTION/CONCLUSION section headers. Content appears to be substantive FPPC guidance on partnership reporting rules under Section 82013(c). No formal question section detected but core legal guidance is clearly stated.

QUESTION: How should partners report contributions when they own interests in a partnership, and must they cumulate their personal contributions with partnership contributions for major donor statement filing purposes?

CONCLUSION: Individuals owning a 50 percent or greater interest in a partnership must cumulate their personal contributions with those of the partnership for purposes of determining whether major donor statements must be filed. One Form 461 should be filed containing the names of the partnership and the partner(s). The statement should also indicate which payments were made by each contributor. Partners owning less than 50 percent of a partnership will cumulate their contributions with those of the partnership only if they are acting as a "combination of persons" which is attempting to influence the voters for or against the nomination or election of a candidate or the passage or defeat of a measure.

One Form 461 should be filed containing the names of the partnership and the partner(s). The statement should also indicate which payments were made by each contributor. Partners owning less than 50 percent of a partnership will cumulate their contributions with those of the partnership only if they are acting as a "combination of persons" which is attempting to influence the voters for or against the nomination or election of a candidate or the passage or defeat of a measure.
embedding.summary: FPPC memorandum addressing reporting requirements for partners and partnerships regarding contribution cumulation and Form 461 filing based on ownership percentage thresholds.
